posts.name: Josh Grossman
posts.cooked: With climate change, you can head north or head to the mountains. I’m looking to explore some great mountain wines from the Savoy region of France. Any favorites?

posts.name: Hank Victor
posts.cooked: Lambert de Seyssel for traditional method sparkling wines. KL imports it.

posts.name: Richard T r i m p i
posts.cooked:
Franck Peillot - Dressner import, Bugey. Southern tip of the Savoie. Always enjoy his Altesse.

Belluard and Peron are worth seeking out and trying.

RT
posts.display_username: Richard T r i m p i

posts.name: Josh Grossman
posts.cooked:
Franck Peillot - Dressner import, Bugey. Southern tip of the Savoie. Always enjoy his Altesse.

Belluard and Peron are worth seeking out and trying.

RT

Thanks and perfect. The only thing I’ve tried was Domaine Dupasquier Altesse Roussette de Savoie Marestel–but it was wonderful and a screaming deal.
We have done well with Domaine Marc Portaz from North Berkley imports. I also like Rondeau Cerdon Bugey from Charles Neal selections.

link_counts.title: Domaine Marc Portaz | North Berkeley Imports

posts.name: Otto Forsberg
posts.cooked:
Although Bugey wines are a great suggestion, they are not part of Savoie anymore!

Nevertheless, a place definitely worth a visit, since the easternmost vineyards of Bugey are only a stone’s throw away from the westernmost vineyards of Savoie.

posts.name: Cris Whetstone
posts.cooked:
I’m a big fan of wines from Apremont. Similar to Muscadet in terms of build and value but still out from the hipster eye. They are made from the Jacquère variety.

Three producers I’ve had very good wines from:

Marc Portaz
Jean Masson
R&B Bernard

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: I love both Curtet and Ardoisières!

posts.name: Mark Henderson
posts.cooked: Have seen very little but very much enjoyed Belluard.

posts.name: Dav3_Dyr0ff
posts.cooked: On the bargain end of the spectrum, Garagiste brings in multiple bottlings from Domaine de L’Idylle. Most under $20, many under $15 (going from memory). The mondeuse-based reds are as enjoyable to me, in a very different style, as just about anything else out there at their price point.

posts.name: Karl K@zaks
posts.cooked:
Some years ago went through some stunning Altesse from Gilles Berlioz.

Have been told he no longer exports to the US (at least I can’t find it in Virginia).

posts.name: Josh Grossman
posts.cooked:
I love both Curtet and Ardoisières!

These look great. Thanks!
